objective: Prove CodeGraph has moved from governance evidence into development-state business task execution by validating a real GFIS task intake, scoped implementation evidence, test fallback selection, and measurable scan reduction.

benefit_interpretation.coverage_value: CodeGraph is mandatory in the task intake gate and the real GFIS candidate evidence.
benefit_interpretation.cost_value: The task intake narrows an 80-file manual scan baseline to 2 CodeGraph candidate files before implementation.
benefit_interpretation.quality_value: Implementation boundaries constrain allowed files and record zero changed files outside allowed scope in the authorized candidate.
benefit_interpretation.test_value: When affectedTests=[] and dependents=0, fallback tests and fallback_reason are required and validated.
benefit_interpretation.governance_value: The benefit is development-state evidence only; it does not complete GFIS business implementation or runtime SOP.

remaining_blockers: Real receipt files, customer confirmation, signed completion documents, or equivalent formal originals are still required before business completion can be claimed
Existing GFIS runtime SOP failure remains: FAIL: KDS coverage must not have missing controlled sources
